title: Phase A · Commit A: CLI command layer v1.x cleanup
description: Remove all v1.x dead code paths from the CLI command layer. Specifically: (1) Delete `packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts` (standalone command obviated by 4-stage init pipeline). (2) In `packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts`: delete `buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown` function + ALL callers; remove all `.fabric/rules/` path constants and dir creation/check. (3) In `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts`: confirm writes go to `.fabric/knowledge/`; remove any residual `.fabric/rules/` writes. (4) In `packages/cli/src/scanner/forensic.ts`: remove `.fabric/rules/` scanning (forensic should only know source-code facts, not fabric self-state). This is the user-facing entry-point cleanup — must be airtight before tests adapt.

files.change: Delete entire file — standalone v1 command obviated by 4-stage init pipeline in v2
Delete buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown function and all callers; remove all .fabric/rules/ path constants, dir creation calls, and existence checks
Confirm scan writes target .fabric/knowledge/; remove any residual .fabric/rules/ writes
Remove .fabric/rules/ scanning — forensic must only inspect source-code facts, not fabric self-state

implementation: Run `grep -rn '\.fabric/rules\|INITIAL_TAXONOMY\|buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown' packages/cli/src/commands/ packages/cli/src/scanner/` to enumerate all mention sites
Delete packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts and remove its registration from CLI command dispatcher (likely packages/cli/src/index.ts)

In init.ts: locate buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown definition + each call site, delete function and inline call expressions; remove .fabric/rules/ path constants (e.g. RULES_DIR), and corresponding mkdir/exists calls
In scan.ts: verify outputDir / target paths point to .fabric/knowledge/{type}/; delete any leftover .fabric/rules/ branch
In scanner/forensic.ts: remove any forensic scan branch that reads .fabric/rules/ — forensic input is source code only, not fabric self-state
Run `pnpm build` (CLI package) to confirm no broken imports / dead references
Run `grep -rn '\.fabric/rules\|INITIAL_TAXONOMY\|buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown\|bootstrap' packages/cli/src/commands/ packages/cli/src/scanner/` and confirm output is empty

reference.pattern: v1.x dead code removal — clean-slate refactor (zero users, no migration tax)
reference.examples: Follow rc.1 commit aaf9c2c clean rebrand pattern — hard delete, no shims/legacy aliases

criteria: File `packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts` does not exist (verified by `test ! -f packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts`)
No matches for `buildInitialTaxonomyMarkdown` in `packages/cli/src/` (grep returns empty)
No matches for `\.fabric/rules` in `packages/cli/src/commands/` or `packages/cli/src/scanner/` (grep returns empty)
No matches for `INITIAL_TAXONOMY` in `packages/cli/src/commands/` (grep returns empty)
`pnpm --filter @fabric/cli build` exits 0 with no broken-import errors
Bootstrap command is unregistered from CLI dispatcher (running `fab bootstrap` errors as unknown command)

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: server-side service layer changes (handled by TASK-002), template changes (handled by TASK-003), test updates (handled by TASK-004). Do NOT touch packages/server/, packages/cli/templates/, or any *.test.ts files in this task.

rationale.chosen_approach: Hard delete v1 dead code in CLI command layer as an isolated commit, with zero shims or legacy aliases
alternatives_considered: Bundle CLI + server cleanup into one commit
decision_factors: Atomic commit scope (1 layer = 1 commit) for revertability
Isolation enables parallel work on TASK-002/003
rationale.tradeoffs: Three separate commits (A/B/C) instead of one — slight ceremony cost for much cleaner history and parallel-safe execution

manual_checks: Manually invoke `node packages/cli/dist/index.js bootstrap` — must error 'unknown command'
Manually invoke `node packages/cli/dist/index.js init --help` — must NOT mention .fabric/rules/
success_metrics: All grep gates from convergence pass
